description: Unified bundle combining Ecosystem_MAX and Governance_Economy_MAX kernels for AMOS/UBI usage.

meta.name: AMOS_Ecosystem_Kernel_Engine_vInfinity_SUPER
meta.author: Trang Phan
meta.description: Deterministic ecosystem kernel+engine that exhaustively covers ecosystem strategy, design, governance, operations, and policy. Zero-gap routing across platforms, alliances, data/infra ecosystems, PPP, geo-strategy, and climate, benchmarked against global best practice.

dimensions_32.name: Strategic_Coherence
dimensions_32.description: Clarity and consistency of ecosystem purpose, role, and boundaries across participants.

dimensions_32.name: Participant_Value_Fit
dimensions_32.description: Strength of value propositions for each participant type and role.

dimensions_32.name: Incentive_Alignment
dimensions_32.description: Match between desired behaviours and economic / non-economic incentives.

dimensions_32.name: Governance_Clarity
dimensions_32.description: Definition of decision rights, rules, escalation paths, and enforcement.

dimensions_32.name: Interoperability_and_Standards
dimensions_32.description: Technical, data, and process interoperability across participants.

dimensions_32.name: Data_Governance
dimensions_32.description: Ownership, access, usage rights, consent, lineage, and auditability of data flows.

dimensions_32.name: Legal_and_Regulatory_Risk
dimensions_32.description: Exposure to competition law, privacy, AML/KYC, sector regulation, sanctions, and cross-border rules.

dimensions_32.name: Economic_Viability
dimensions_32.description: Revenue, cost, margin pools, and long-term profitability across the ecosystem.

dimensions_32.name: Capital_and_Funding_Structure
dimensions_32.description: Investment flows, risk allocation, and capital structure robustness.

dimensions_32.name: Operational_Resilience
dimensions_32.description: Ability to withstand shocks, outages, and failures without systemic collapse.

dimensions_32.name: Redundancy_and_Diversity
dimensions_32.description: Diversity of participants, routes, infrastructure, and options to avoid single points of failure.

dimensions_32.name: Trust_and_Reputation
dimensions_32.description: Perceived fairness, reliability, and integrity of ecosystem rules and operators.

dimensions_32.name: Transparency_and_Observability
dimensions_32.description: Visibility into rules, flows, metrics, and compliance for participants and regulators.

dimensions_32.name: Innovation_Velocity
dimensions_32.description: Rate of new product, service, and capability creation within the ecosystem.

dimensions_32.name: Talent_and_Knowledge_Flows
dimensions_32.description: Movement of skills, know-how, and learning loops across entities.

dimensions_32.name: Policy_Compatibility
dimensions_32.description: Alignment with national, regional, and international policy objectives.

dimensions_32.name: Climate_and_Externalities
dimensions_32.description: Impact on emissions, biodiversity, and social externalities; mitigation mechanisms.

dimensions_32.name: Inclusion_and_Access
dimensions_32.description: Accessibility for SMEs, emerging markets, vulnerable groups, and new entrants.

dimensions_32.name: Security_and_Abuse_Potential
dimensions_32.description: Cyber, fraud, misuse, and concentration-of-power risks.

dimensions_32.name: Dispute_Resolution_and_Exit
dimensions_32.description: Mechanisms for conflict resolution, exit, and restructuring without system collapse.

dimensions_32.name: Measurement_and_KPIs
dimensions_32.description: Quality and completeness of ecosystem-wide metrics and dashboards.

dimensions_32.name: Scenario_and_Shock_Readiness
dimensions_32.description: Ability to run, understand, and act on multi-shock scenarios.

dimensions_32.name: Cross_Ecosystem_Compatibility
dimensions_32.description: Ability to interact or interconnect with other ecosystems and infrastructures.

dimensions_32.name: Platform_Tech_Suitability
dimensions_32.description: Fitness of the platform / tech stack for scale, latency, compliance, and resilience.

dimensions_32.name: Rollout_and_Adoption_Path
dimensions_32.description: Realistic, staged adoption and rollout path with incentives and support.

dimensions_32.name: Local_Global_Balance
dimensions_32.description: Balance between local adaptation and global consistency.

dimensions_32.name: Geo_Strategic_Implications
dimensions_32.description: Impacts on national security, alliances, dependencies, and strategic autonomy.

dimensions_32.name: Systemic_Risk_Contribution
dimensions_32.description: Degree to which ecosystem increases or mitigates systemic risk.

dimensions_32.name: Ethical_and_Human_Rights_Alignment
dimensions_32.description: Consistency with baseline ethical, human rights, and fairness constraints.

dimensions_32.name: Change_Management_Capability
dimensions_32.description: Readiness to evolve governance, rules, and membership without destabilisation.

dimensions_32.name: Evidence_Strength
dimensions_32.description: Depth and reliability of data and case studies supporting the design choices.

dimensions_32.name: Implementation_Readiness
dimensions_32.description: Clarity of next steps, owners, resources, and timelines for execution.

tensor.notes: Any ecosystem scenario, design, or governance question is represented as a cell in this tensor defined by scope, relationship, value flows, maturity, governance model, geography, time, risk, diversity, regulation, competition, and climate class.

F_normalize_task.logic: Parse text into structured fields: objective, scope, geography, actors, time_horizon, risk_focus, ecosystem_type.

F_map_axes.logic: Select values for all 12 axes based on structured input and safe defaults.

F_dimension_profile.logic: Set importance profile across 32 dimensions for this task.

F_stateframe.logic: Instantiate local kernel state used by engine modes.

governance.anchor_to_MetaKernel_01: Deterministic law, identity continuity, boundary compliance mandatory.

ecosystem_diagnosis.description: Full-structure diagnosis of an existing or proposed ecosystem.

ecosystem_design.description: Design or redesign of ecosystem strategy, structure, and governance.

ecosystem_governance.description: Definition or refinement of ecosystem governance, rules, and operating model.

ecosystem_simulation.description: Scenario and shock analysis for ecosystem resilience and risk.

ecosystem_negotiation.description: Multi-party negotiation framing for ecosystem creation or restructuring.

ecosystem_unwind.description: Safe restructuring, exit, or wind-down of ecosystems.

notes: Ecosystem_Strategy_Engine_vInfinity supplies detailed playbooks and scenarios.
BizFin kernel supplies macro, sector, and market tensor for economic backbones.
Product, Ops, Risk, Policy, and Negotiation kernels supply deep domain sub-logic for each ecosystem aspect.

global_reference_frameworks: Top-tier consulting ecosystem and platform strategy playbooks (McKinsey, BCG, Bain - conceptual coverage).
Big tech platform and partner network operating models (e.g., hyperscalers, app stores, payment networks - structural patterns).
Multilateral and national data space / digital market initiatives (EU data spaces, open banking, national digital ID ecosystems - policy and governance patterns).
Critical infrastructure and payment systems governance (RTGS, card networks, cross-border payment messaging - resilience and systemic risk patterns).

strategy_and_design.notes: Covers full lifecycle from concept to unwind across platform, data, infra, PPP, and climate ecosystems.

governance_and_policy.notes: Integrates competition law, data governance, AML/KYC, policy, and geo-strategy kernels.

economics_and_finance.notes: Backed by BizFin kernel for TAM/SAM/SOM, macro-micro linkage, and capital flows.

operations_and_resilience.notes: Uses Ops, Risk, Audit, Engineering Math, and Tech kernels for infra and ops risk.

negotiation_and_diplomacy.notes: Negotiation and policy kernels support alliance and multi-state ecosystems.

limitations: Does not contain proprietary or confidential playbook content; it encodes structural coverage instead.
All benchmarks are conceptual; users must supply local data, law, and policy specifics.

notes: The engine does not materialise all virtual layers; it defines the lawful tensor so any concrete ecosystem scenario can be instantiated on demand.
Kernel+engine composition ensures zero structural gaps across ecosystem-related reasoning when combined with referenced AMOS engines.

meta.name: Governance_Economy_MAX
meta.author: Trang Phan + AMOS_CORE
meta.description: MAX kernel for Governance Economy sectors linked to TTS, TPE, and AMOS Universe.
meta.created_from: manual synthesis in chat based on Ecosystem_MAX.json pattern

sectors_5.name: Real Economy
sectors_5.scope: Production, trade and services in physical goods and day-to-day services.

sectors_5.name: Financial System
sectors_5.scope: Money, credit, capital markets, banking and shadow finance.

sectors_5.name: Governance & Institutions
sectors_5.scope: States, regulators, courts, central banks, rule-making bodies.

sectors_5.name: Infrastructure & Energy
sectors_5.scope: Physical grids, logistics, energy systems, digital infra.

sectors_5.name: Information & Culture
sectors_5.scope: Media, platforms, education, social narratives and norms.

tts_linkage.omega: Measure of overload and complexity in each sector (debt, capacity, constraints).
tts_linkage.h: Cohesion within and between actors in a sector (trust, alignment, rule adherence).
tts_linkage.f: Fragmentation of interests, actors, data and incentives.
tts_linkage.s: Shock sensitivity and propagation speed from local event to whole system.

tpe_linkage.r: Renewal outcome; sector reconfigures but remains functional.
tpe_linkage.t: Termination outcome; sector node is shut, nationalised or exits.
tpe_linkage.a: Absorption outcome; sector absorbed into stronger actor or bloc.
tpe_linkage.sg: Stagnation outcome; high friction, low productivity, chronic stress.

kernel_to_layer_mapping.laws: Core structural laws for sectors and state spaces.
kernel_to_layer_mapping.policies: Applied rules and interventions per sector.
kernel_to_layer_mapping.operations: Daily behaviours, flows and resource movements.

axes.description: TSS cycle phase of the sector.
Overload level in sector (stress, constraints, leverage).
Cohesion level inside sector and with governance.
Fragmentation level of actors, data and incentives.
Dominant governance mode for this sector state.
Primary time horizon used for decisions.
Window when interventions are still effective.
AMOS layer where the current leverage is highest.

R.label: Renewal
R.description: System restructures with higher integrity and lower Omega, with sectors rebalanced.

T.label: Termination
T.description: System or sector node deliberately shut down, defaulted, or written off.

A.label: Absorption
A.description: System or sector absorbed into a stronger actor, bloc, or governance layer.

Sg.label: Stagnation
Sg.description: System remains formally in place but with low productivity and chronic high Omega.

transition_rules: If AX03_omega_level = critical AND AX04_cohesion_h = low AND AX05_fragmentation_f = high THEN AX10_risk_state -> crisis.
If AX02_cycle_state = C3_peak AND AX03_omega_level = high AND AX05_fragmentation_f = rising THEN AX02_cycle_state -> C4_fragment.
If AX10_risk_state = crisis AND no effective intervention in AX11_intervention_window = actionable THEN projected outcome in {T, A, Sg}.
If targeted interventions reduce AX03_omega_level by 1 band AND increase AX04_cohesion_h by 1 band THEN probability of R increases.
If AX07_governance_mode = hybrid AND AX08_ownership_regime = mixed AND AX04_cohesion_h = high THEN sector is resilient to isolated shocks.
If AX07_governance_mode = informal AND AX08_ownership_regime = criminal AND AX03_omega_level >= high THEN systemic spillover risk rises to crisis.
If AX12_amos_layer = laws AND reforms align incentives with TTS, THEN long-term Omega trend decreases across AX01 sector family.
If AX12_amos_layer = culture AND narratives reduce polarisation, THEN AX04_cohesion_h increases and AX05_fragmentation_f decreases.

state_indices.projected_outcome: R or Sg depending on intervention.

energy_bands.medium: Elevated but manageable stress; early warning.
energy_bands.high: High stress with visible distortions in macro variables.
energy_bands.critical: System near tipping point; small shocks can trigger C5–C6.

collapse_windows.short_term: 0–24 months for fast-moving financial and political crises.
collapse_windows.medium_term: 2–7 years for structural rebalancing, debt and regime shifts.
collapse_windows.long_term: 7–20 years for demographic, climate and institutional redesign effects.

functions.score_sector_state: Input = concrete sector snapshot. Map to axes, compute Omega/H/F/S band and risk_state.
functions.predict_transition: Given initial state and policy set, estimate most probable next C-state and TPE outcome.
functions.design_intervention: Search AX12_amos_layer combinations that move system from high/critical Omega to medium/low.
functions.allocate_capital: Rank sectors and states by risk-adjusted opportunity under R/T/A/Sg probabilities.

policies.integrity_first: Always design interventions that reduce Omega without creating hidden fragmentation or off-balance risks.
policies.no_free_lunch: Every short-term Omega reduction must be checked for long-term cost in other sectors or time horizons.
policies.governance_alignment: Align sector incentives with TTS variables so that self-interest reduces Omega and fragmentation.
policies.bounded_claims: System outputs must expose sources of uncertainty, not fake deterministic precision.

integrity_checks: Axes must be mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive for the use-case.
Every state index used in products must be traceable back to human-auditable parameters.
No black-box weights in high-stakes governance decisions without explanation.
edge_cases: Failed states where SEC03 and SEC02 collapse together.
Hyper-financialised economies with SEC02 dominating SEC01 and SEC04.
Authoritarian regimes with high Omega but artificially high short-term cohesion.

prompts.state_encoding_prompt: Given a country/sector description, encode it into AX01–AX12 axes and return Omega/H/F/S and risk_state.
prompts.policy_design_prompt: Given a high-risk state, propose 3–5 interventions at laws/policies/operations/behaviour/culture layers that move the system toward Renewal.
prompts.capital_allocation_prompt: Rank sectors by expected R/T/A/Sg outcome under current global cycles and suggest risk-aware allocations.
